name: Webcam Pixel Grid
summary: An interactive webcam visualization that renders real-time video as an elevated 3D pixel grid with motion detection.
detailedDescription: Webcam Pixel Grid captures live webcam feed and renders it as a grid of 3D elevated cells (default 64x48) using canvas rendering. The component accesses webcam via getUserMedia API, processes video frames to extract pixel colors, and calculates motion by comparing consecutive frames. Each cell's elevation is determined by motion intensity - more movement creates higher elevation with smooth transitions using lerp smoothing (default 0.1 factor). The component supports two color modes: 'webcam' uses actual pixel colors from the feed, while 'monochrome' applies a single configurable color. Visual customization includes gap ratio between cells, border color/opacity, background color, mirror mode, and invert colors option. Motion sensitivity (0-1) controls how responsive elevation is to movement. The rendering pipeline uses three canvases: video element for webcam input, processing canvas for frame analysis, and display canvas for final pixel grid output. Elevation is rendered using canvas transformations with configurable max elevation (default 15px). Error handling includes callbacks for webcam access denial. This creates an interactive, reactive visualization perfect for music visualizers, motion-activated displays, or creative webcam effects.
purpose: Visualize webcam feed as 3D elevated pixel grid
Detect and display motion through cell elevation
Create interactive real-time video art installations
behavior: Requests webcam access via getUserMedia API
Compares consecutive frames for motion detection
Calculates cell elevation based on motion intensity
Renders grid with configurable gap and borders
visualCharacteristics: Actual pixel colors or monochrome mode
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
